qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ForwardAuth RemoveConnectionHeaders allows arbitrary header deletion from authentication requests
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns ForwardAuth HTTP/1.1 Connection-header processing in pkg/middlewares/auth/connectionheader.go, while the advisory concerns HTTP/2 frames causing a Traefik server panic. The affected component, code path, root cause, and impact do not match.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying final finding identifies the advisory-described HTTP/2 frame handling panic or the related CWE-476 root cause in Traefik. The only qualifying final finding is an unrelated ForwardAuth header-deletion issue.
evidence: The advisory summary is "Traefik: HTTP/2 frames can cause a running server to panic" and its CVSS vector records only subsequent-system availability impact.
The advisory affects github.com/traefik/traefik/v2 before 2.11.40 and github.com/traefik/traefik/v3 before 3.6.10, with CWE-476.
The session manifest lists only one qualifying final finding: forwardauth-connection-header-manipulation.
That qualifying finding identifies pkg/middlewares/auth/connectionheader.go and pkg/middlewares/auth/forward.go header deletion in ForwardAuth, not HTTP/2 frame parsing or panic behavior.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matched the advisory root cause, so runtime tiering is not applicable.
